name: Lampung
history: Lampung, located at the southern tip of Sumatra, has a rich maritime history shaped by its position along ancient trade routes connecting the Indian Ocean to the Java Sea. The region was historically inhabited by indigenous Lampung peoples who developed their own distinct culture and sultanates before falling under Dutch colonial rule in the late 19th century. During Indonesia's independence struggle, Lampung played a significant role as a center of resistance against colonial forces. The province developed further during the post-independence era, particularly through transmigration programs that brought settlers from other Indonesian islands.
culturalNotes: Lampung is known for its distinctive Lampung people, who maintain unique traditions including the famous Lampung weaving and batik patterns that reflect the region's artistic heritage. The province celebrates its cultural identity through events like the Krakatau Festival and maintains strong traditions of music, dance, and oral storytelling that blend indigenous practices with influences from neighboring Javanese and Sundanese cultures. The province's character is marked by its multicultural composition, with transmigration having created a diverse society while the indigenous Lampung traditions remain deeply rooted in the local identity.
bestTimeToVisit: Best visited from May to September during the dry season.
languageNotes: The primary languages spoken in Lampung are Indonesian and the Lampung language, an Austronesian language native to the region with several dialects including Abung and Pekon. Useful phrases include 'Selamat pagi' for good morning and 'Terima kasih' for thank you, which are standard Indonesian expressions understood throughout the province.

places.name: Bandar Lampung
places.description: The capital and largest city of Lampung Province, serving as the gateway to the region with modern amenities, markets, and coastal views. It offers a blend of urban life and access to nearby natural attractions, making it an essential base for exploring the province.

activities.detail: A modern seaside promenade with walking paths, parks, and views of the Lampung Strait, ideal for sunset walks and local community gatherings.
An educational museum dedicated to the history and geology of Krakatau volcano, featuring artifacts, photographs, and interactive displays about the catastrophic 1883 eruption.
A sandy beach located just outside the city center offering swimming, local food stalls, and views across the strait to Sumatra's coastal landscape.

activities.text: Way Kambas National Park Day Trip
activities.detail: An accessible wildlife sanctuary about 90 minutes from the city hosting Sumatran elephants, rhinos, and tigers in their natural habitat with guided tours available.
The main traditional market in the city center where locals shop for fresh produce, spices, textiles, and handicrafts, offering an authentic glimpse into daily urban life.
A mountain national park about two hours from the city featuring rainforest hikes, waterfalls, and endemic wildlife including orangutans and clouded leopards.

food.detail: A savory cake made from fish and tapioca flour served with a rich, spicy gravy called cuko, originating from nearby Palembang but widely enjoyed throughout Bandar Lampung.
A traditional Lampung dish of seasoned minced fish mixed with grated coconut and spices, typically grilled in banana leaves and served with rice and sambal.
Fresh grilled fish, prawns, and squid prepared with local spices at the many open-air seafood stalls along Tanjung Pesona, best enjoyed in the evening.
A aromatic fish-head curry cooked with coconut milk, turmeric, and galangal that is a beloved comfort food and specialty throughout the region.
Grilled fish paste mixed with spices and wrapped in banana leaves, a popular street snack that can be found at markets and food carts throughout the city.

culture.detail: Lampung has strong maritime heritage; showing respect to local fishermen and understanding their customs regarding beach areas will enhance interactions.
While Bandar Lampung is relatively cosmopolitan, wearing modest clothing especially in traditional markets and religious areas shows respect for local values.
Though some English is spoken in tourist areas, speaking basic Indonesian (like 'Terima kasih' for thank you) is appreciated and helps with street vendors and local interactions.
Locals commonly gather at Tanjung Pesona and waterfront areas in the early evening for leisurely walks and socializing; joining this relaxed atmosphere offers authentic local experience.

places.name: Krakatoa
places.description: One of the world's most famous volcanic islands, partially destroyed in the catastrophic 1883 eruption and still home to an active volcano called Anak Krakatau. The dramatic landscape and geological significance make it one of Indonesia's most iconic and rewarding destinations.

activities.detail: Hike to the summit of the active volcanic cone that emerged from the sea after the 1883 eruption, offering views of steaming vents and crater formations.
Explore the vibrant coral reefs and tropical fish populations that have colonized the shallow waters surrounding the volcanic islands.
Trek through the remnants of the original pre-1883 crater rim on Rakata, the largest remaining island, to understand the scale of the catastrophic eruption.
Watch the sun set over the volcanic caldera while observing the active Anak Krakatau cone in the distance, a dramatic natural spectacle.
Document the unique geological features including pumice deposits, volcanic rock formations, and the contrast between barren volcanic slopes and encroaching vegetation.
Visit the locations most affected by the catastrophic eruption that killed over 36,000 people, accessible via boat tours from nearby towns.

food.detail: Fresh-caught fish from the Sunda Strait grilled over charcoal with local spices and served with sambal, a staple in nearby coastal villages.
Local blue crabs from the surrounding waters cooked in rich coconut-based curry with turmeric, galangal, and chilies.
Fragrant turmeric-infused rice cooked in coconut milk, typically served as an accompaniment to grilled seafood dishes.
Golden-fried potato croquettes seasoned with garlic and onion, commonly offered as a side dish in local warungs and accommodations.
A rich and spicy soup from the Lampung region featuring beef, turmeric, and traditional herbs, warming and satisfying after volcanic trekking.

culture.detail: Local communities view Krakatoa with spiritual significance as a site of great natural power; approach the volcano with reverence and follow guide instructions carefully.
All visits to Anak Krakatau require a licensed local guide due to ongoing volcanic activity and safety considerations; never attempt independent exploration.

Access to Krakatoa is highly dependent on sea conditions and volcanic activity status; plan flexibility into your itinerary as tours may be cancelled with little notice during monsoon season or increased volcanic activity.
Support local boatmen and guides from nearby villages like Carita and Labuan who depend on tourism; purchase supplies from local markets before departing for the islands.

places.name: Way Kambas National Park
places.description: One of Sumatra's largest national parks, known as a sanctuary for endangered Sumatran elephants and other wildlife including rhinos and tigers. The park offers jungle trekking, elephant encounters, and rich biodiversity that appeals to nature lovers and wildlife enthusiasts.

activities.detail: Ride on the back of trained Sumatran elephants through jungle trails to observe their natural habitat and behavior up close.
Trek through dense rainforest on marked paths to spot wildlife including birds, gibbons, and occasionally rhinos in their native environment.

activities.text: Wildlife Viewing at Elephant Training Centre
activities.detail: Visit the park's elephant sanctuary where you can observe rescued and trained elephants being cared for and participating in educational programs.
Take a boat ride along the Way Kambas River to spot crocodiles, water birds, and riverside wildlife in their natural setting.
Explore the park after dark with a guide to encounter nocturnal animals and experience the jungle's nighttime ecosystem.
Identify over 300 bird species including eagles, hornbills, and kingfishers along designated birding routes throughout the park.

food.detail: A rich, spiced curry made with freshwater fish from local rivers, cooked with coconut milk and traditional Lampung spices.
A savory snack of fish cake and tapioca served with a tangy vinegar-based sauce, popular throughout the Lampung region.
Fragrant yellow rice cooked with turmeric, coconut milk, and local spices, often served at park lodges and nearby villages.
A fiery condiment made from fermented durian mixed with chili peppers, representing authentic Sumatran flavor and heat.

food.text: Fresh Tropical Fruit at Local Markets
food.detail: Sample mangosteen, rambutan, and durian from nearby village markets that supply seasonal produce to park accommodations.

culture.detail: Always use park-approved guides who understand elephant behavior, wildlife safety protocols, and can share authentic stories about conservation efforts.
Maintain safe distances from animals and follow your guide's instructions strictly, as this protects both you and the endangered species.
Plan your visit between May and September when trails are most accessible and wildlife viewing is optimal, avoiding the heavy rain months.
Purchase tickets and services directly through official park channels to ensure your money supports elephant rescue and habitat protection programs.

places.name: Liwa
places.description: A charming highland town in western Lampung known for its cool climate, traditional Lampung culture, and scenic mountain scenery. It serves as an excellent base for exploring the Bukit Barisan mountain range and experiencing authentic rural Lampung life.

activities.detail: Explore the mountain ridges surrounding Liwa with guided treks offering views of forest canopy, tea plantations, and occasional wildlife sightings.
Tour working tea estates on the hillsides around Liwa to learn about cultivation and processing while enjoying fresh mountain air.
Wander through the bustling traditional market where locals sell fresh vegetables, spices, and handicrafts reflecting daily Lampung life.
Trek to scenic overlooks above the Way Kanan River valley to experience the landscape that defines the Liwa region.
Spend time in family-run guesthouses where hosts teach visitors about local customs, agriculture, and evening communal meals.

food.detail: A rich, spiced stew made with young jackfruit, coconut milk, and turmeric that represents classic Lampung highland cuisine.
Crispy fried catfish from local rivers served with sambal and fresh lime, a staple protein in Liwa's diet.
Sticky rice paired with a mild coconut vegetable curry featuring local greens, pumpkin, and beans from mountain gardens.
Fried bananas endemic to the highland region, often dusted with sugar and served as a snack or dessert.
Join plantation workers for traditional tea preparation rituals and taste freshly brewed leaf tea straight from the estate.

culture.detail: In Lampung tradition, elders are consulted on important decisions and should be greeted first in any social setting.
Conservative dress is valued in Liwa's traditional communities; cover shoulders and knees when visiting markets, homestays, and cultural sites.
Accept invitations to eat with locals, as sharing food is a core part of Lampung hospitality and relationship-building.
Using phrases like 'Halo pian' (hello) and 'Terima kasih' (thank you) shows respect and earns genuine warmth from residents.

places.name: Labuhan Ratu
places.description: A scenic coastal town known for its beautiful beaches and fishing harbor with colorful traditional boats. It offers a more relaxed, authentic coastal experience compared to larger cities and is popular for seafood dining and marine exploration.

activities.detail: Stroll through the working harbor at dawn to see traditional wooden boats being prepared and the daily catch being unloaded by local fishermen.
Walk along the sandy shores of Labuhan Ratu Beach to observe local life, swim in calm waters, and watch the sunset over the fishing boats.
Join local boat operators for snorkeling trips to nearby coral reefs and small islands to see tropical fish and marine ecosystems.
Visit the early morning fish market to see the variety of freshly caught seafood and interact with vendors selling local produce and dried goods.
Take a guided boat through the nearby mangrove forests to observe wildlife, learn about the ecosystem, and experience the natural brackish waterways.
Wander through residential areas and small villages surrounding the harbor to see traditional stilt houses and daily village life.

food.detail: Fresh-caught fish grilled over charcoal and served with sambal, lime, and rice at beachside warungs—a Labuhan Ratu specialty using the day's catch.
A warming broth made with mixed fresh seafood, turmeric, and local herbs, typically served with rice or bread at family-run coastal restaurants.
Large fresh prawns fried until crispy and served with spiced dipping sauces, a popular dish at night markets and seafood stalls.
Tender squid cooked in a fiery red chili and garlic sauce, commonly found at warung near the harbor where fishermen eat.
Yellow turmeric rice cooked with coconut milk and served alongside grilled or fried seafood, a festive coastal meal.

culture.detail: Visit the harbor and fish market before 7 AM to experience the most vibrant fishing activity and freshest seafood selection of the day.
Ask permission before photographing boats or fishermen at work, as many depend on fishing for their livelihood and prefer to control their image.
Bring Indonesian Rupiah in smaller denominations as many beachside warungs and market vendors do not accept cards or have limited change.
Wear respectful clothing when walking through residential areas and villages, keeping shoulders and knees covered to honor local Islamic customs.

places.name: Tanjung Karang
places.description: The waterfront district of Bandar Lampung featuring bustling markets, seafood restaurants, and traditional wooden fishing boats. It's the heart of local life and commerce, offering an authentic glimpse into Lampung's maritime culture and daily activities.

activities.detail: Wander through the early morning bustling fish market to witness fishermen unloading their daily catch and experience the vibrant trading atmosphere.
Visit the waterfront workshops where local craftsmen handcraft traditional wooden fishing boats using techniques passed down through generations.
Sample fresh grilled fish and seafood dishes at open-air restaurants overlooking the harbor while observing the daily maritime activity.
Capture the picturesque scene of colorful traditional fishing boats returning to harbor as the sun sets over the Lampung Strait.

activities.text: Visit Pasar Tanjung Karang Night Market
activities.detail: Browse local goods, street food stalls, and handicrafts at the evening market that reflects authentic Bandar Lampung commerce and community life.

activities.text: Take a Boat Tour of the Harbor
activities.detail: Hire a local boatman for a short tour around the harbor to observe fishing operations and get a water-level perspective of maritime life.

food.detail: A traditional Lampung dish of fresh fish simmered in turmeric and spice-infused broth, commonly served at waterfront warung with steamed rice.
Fresh-caught fish grilled over charcoal and served with fiery sambal belacan, a staple preparation at Tanjung Karang's seafood restaurants.
Grilled shrimp or squid skewers marinated in spices and served with peanut sauce, popular street food from evening market vendors.
Steamed or grilled spiced fish paste wrapped in banana leaves, a beloved local snack sold by market vendors throughout the waterfront.
A rich, aromatic curry featuring fish head cooked with coconut milk and spices, prized by locals for its tender meat and flavorful broth.

culture.text: Visit Early Morning for Authentic Experience
culture.detail: Arrive before 7 AM to witness the market's peak activity when fishing boats return and traders are most active.
Ask permission before photographing boats or fishermen, as many are superstitious about cameras and prefer respectful interaction.
Haggling is expected but should be done lightheartedly and respectfully; a friendly demeanor will earn better prices and interactions.
Speaking even simple phrases like 'Selamat pagi' (good morning) and 'Terima kasih' (thank you) greatly enhances rapport with local vendors and fishermen.

places.name: Labuhan Lombok
places.description: A small coastal fishing village with pristine beaches and excellent seafood, located south of Bandar Lampung. The area is known for its unspoiled natural beauty and is becoming increasingly popular with travelers seeking quieter beach experiences.

activities.detail: Stroll along the pristine shoreline at dawn to observe local fishermen unloading their catch and enjoy the calm waters before the day gets busy.
Explore the shallow coral formations just offshore where tropical fish species are abundant and visibility is excellent during calm weather.
Visit the early morning fish market to see the variety of local catches, interact with fishermen, and select fresh seafood for your meals.
Paddle through nearby mangrove channels to spot birds, monitor lizards, and experience the ecosystem that supports the local fishing industry.
Find a quiet spot on the beach during late afternoon to watch the sun set over the Indian Ocean with minimal crowds.
Stay with a local fishing family to learn about daily village life, traditional boat-building techniques, and authentic coastal living.

food.detail: Whole fish caught that morning, grilled over charcoal with minimal seasoning to let the fresh flavor shine, served with sambal and lime.
A pungent local shrimp paste condiment blended with fresh chilies, garlic, and lime that accompanies nearly every meal in the village.
Jumbo local shrimp fried crispy with turmeric and garlic, often served as a special dish at beachfront warungs.
A warming broth made from fish stock, turmeric, galangal, and local herbs, typically eaten for breakfast or lunch.
Fresh squid netted daily and quickly fried until tender, seasoned simply with salt, pepper, and served with dipping sauces.

culture.detail: Plan activities around dawn departures and afternoon returns of fishing boats, as these are the village's most important daily rhythms.
While the beach is casual, keep shoulders and knees covered when walking through the village itself to show respect to Muslim residents.
Light haggling is expected at the fish market and with boat operators, but maintain a friendly tone as locals appreciate respectful negotiation.
English is minimal in this village, so greeting locals with 'Selamat pagi' (good morning) and 'Terima kasih' (thank you) will earn genuine smiles and hospitality.

places.name: Sunda Strait
places.description: The narrow waterway separating Java and Sumatra, offering boat tours with stunning views of Krakatoa and the surrounding volcanic landscape. The strait is historically significant and provides spectacular photo opportunities, especially during sunrise and sunset.

activities.detail: Embark on an early morning boat tour from Merak or Bakauheni to witness the iconic volcanic island of Krakatoa emerge from the mist at dawn, with opportunities to photograph the dramatic landscape.
Take a guided boat excursion to Anak Krakatau (Child of Krakatau), the active volcanic island that emerged after the 1883 eruption, where you can hike to viewpoints and observe volcanic activity.
Position yourself at Merak's waterfront area to capture stunning sunset photographs over the strait with Sumatra's coastline as a backdrop.
Explore the coral reefs and marine life around smaller islands in the strait, which offer decent snorkeling and diving opportunities despite the strong currents.
Visit the Krakatoa Museum in Lampung or nearby interpretive centers to learn about the 1883 eruption, its global impact, and the geological history of the region.
Stroll along Bakauheni's beaches to observe local fishing activities, traditional wooden boats, and enjoy fresh seafood at waterfront warungs.

food.detail: Grilled fish prepared with aromatic spices and served with sambal, a specialty of restaurants along the strait that emphasize the fresh catch of the day.
A rich, turmeric-based curry with fish or shrimp, traditionally made by Lampung communities and commonly served with rice at local coastal eateries.
Spicy fried shrimp coated in chili paste and garlic, reflecting the regional preference for bold, fiery flavors in Lampung cuisine.
Spring rolls filled with vegetables or shrimp, often enjoyed as a snack or appetizer at ports and small food stalls around Merak and Bakauheni.
Fresh whole fish, crabs, squid, and shellfish bought directly from the early morning catch and grilled or steamed at nearby food stalls for an authentic experience.

culture.detail: Ask permission before photographing fishermen and their boats, as these are working vessels essential to local livelihoods, and always tip guides generously for their knowledge and time.
Wear sun protection, modest clothing, and bring layers as mornings and evenings can be cool on the water; women should consider a light scarf as a respectful gesture in Muslim-majority areas.
Simple greetings like 'Selamat pagi' (good morning) and 'Terima kasih' (thank you) are greatly appreciated by locals and enhance your interactions at ports and small restaurants.
The strait can experience rough seas and unpredictable weather; consult local guides about safe travel times and always verify boat safety standards before departing.

places.name: Mount Rajabasa
places.description: A scenic volcanic mountain located in southern Lampung offering hiking opportunities and panoramic views of the surrounding region. The mountain is culturally significant and provides adventurous trekkers with challenging trails and rewarding vistas.

activities.detail: Hike the main trail to the 1,324-meter summit for panoramic views of the Sunda Strait, surrounding hills, and clear days may reveal views toward Java.
Walk along the volcanic crater rim to observe the geological formations and understand the mountain's volcanic history.
Trek to the seasonal waterfall on the mountain's slope, which flows best during the rainy season and offers a refreshing stop.
Start before dawn to capture the golden sunrise illuminating the Sunda Strait and casting dramatic shadows across the volcanic landscape.
Meet local Lampung communities in villages at the mountain's base to learn about traditional agriculture and daily life.
Observe endemic and migratory bird species throughout the forested slopes, particularly during migration seasons.

food.detail: A rich, spiced curry dish featuring locally-caught crabs, reflecting Lampung's coastal proximity and use of fresh seafood.
A traditional sour soup made with fresh river or sea fish, tamarind, and local herbs, commonly served in mountain villages.
A spicy condiment made from fermented durian mixed with chili peppers and salt, representing Lampung's unique flavor profile.
Crispy fried grasshoppers seasoned with local spices, a protein-rich snack popular among hikers and local communities.
Fragrant turmeric-infused rice cooked with coconut milk and spices, typically served during local celebrations and gatherings.

culture.detail: The mountain holds spiritual significance for local Lampung communities, so treat natural landmarks and any shrines respectfully and ask permission before photographing.
Employ local guides from nearby villages, which supports the community economically and ensures safer navigation of less obvious trails.
Start early morning hikes respectfully and avoid loud noise to honor the mountain's cultural significance and wildlife habitats.
Simple greetings in Lampung language like 'Halo' and 'Terima kasih' are appreciated by local residents and enhance cultural exchange.

places.name: Semaka
places.description: A traditional Lampung village known for its authentic culture and traditional weaving crafts, particularly the famous songket textiles. Visiting offers insight into indigenous Lampung traditions and the opportunity to support local artisans directly.

activities.detail: Watch skilled artisans at work on traditional wooden looms creating intricate gold and silver thread patterns in the famous Lampung songket textile.
Tour traditional Lampung houses where families have practiced songket weaving for generations and purchase directly from weavers.
Browse the village market for handwoven textiles, batik fabrics, and local crafts while observing daily life and meeting local vendors.
Learn about Lampung indigenous heritage, traditional costumes, and the history of songket weaving through displayed artifacts and exhibits.
Document the distinctive Lampung traditional houses with their boat-shaped roofs and carved wooden details throughout the village.
Witness the traditional natural dyeing techniques using local plants that give Semaka songket its characteristic rich colors.

food.detail: A traditional Lampung fish dish cooked in sour tamarind-based broth with aromatic herbs and spices, served with steamed rice.
A savory Lampung curry made with fish or shrimp cooked in coconut milk with turmeric, galangal, and local spices.
Sweet fried pastry snacks filled with coconut and brown sugar, commonly made by local women and sold at the village market.
Fragrant turmeric rice cooked with coconut milk and local herbs, often served at community gatherings and celebrations.
Fresh chili paste made with locally grown peppers, garlic, and shallots, served as a standard condiment with most Lampung meals.

culture.text: Respect for Artisans and Their Work
culture.detail: Always ask permission before photographing weavers at work and consider purchasing their textiles as fair compensation for their skill and time.
Wear respectful clothing that covers shoulders and knees when visiting homes and workshops, as this is a traditional conservative village.
Greeting locals with 'Selamat pagi' (good morning) and showing interest in their craft will be greatly appreciated and enhance interactions.
Plan your visit around local festivals and celebrations like Lampung Way Kambas Festival to experience authentic cultural performances and traditions.

places.name: Elephant Training Center
places.description: Located near Way Kambas, this center rescues and rehabilitates Sumatran elephants while providing educational experiences for visitors. It combines conservation efforts with the opportunity to observe and interact with these magnificent endangered creatures in a responsible manner.

activities.detail: Hand-feed Sumatran elephants fresh fruits and vegetables while learning about their diet and behavior from trained handlers.
Watch rescued elephants cool off and protect their skin by wallowing in mud pools, a natural behavior essential to their wellbeing.
Join an educational walk through the rehabilitation facilities to understand conservation efforts and individual elephant rescue stories.
Participate in bathing elephants at the water station using brushes and hoses while interacting closely with these gentle giants.
Capture professional-quality photos of elephants in natural settings during feeding and bathing times with guidance on responsible wildlife photography.
Attend a talk by center staff about Sumatran elephant endangered status, habitat loss, and ongoing rescue and rehabilitation programs.
Explore the adjacent protected forest reserve to spot wild elephants and other Sumatran wildlife in their natural habitat.

food.detail: A Palembang specialty of fish cake served with dark sweet and spicy gravy, widely available in local food stalls near the center.
Fragrant turmeric rice cooked with coconut milk and spices, a staple comfort food at regional restaurants in Lampung.
Tender beef curry slow-cooked in rich coconut and spice sauce, commonly served with rice at local warungs.
Stir-fried bitter beans with shrimp paste and chilies, a pungent but beloved local vegetable dish reflecting Lampung cuisine.
Crispy deep-fried tofu served with sambal and peanut sauce, an affordable and popular snack at center cafeterias.
Local mangoes, papayas, and durian available seasonally at nearby markets, showcasing Lampung's abundant produce.

culture.detail: Wear lightweight, modest clothing and closed-toe shoes for safety around elephants; avoid bright colors that may startle animals.
Always follow handler instructions and maintain awareness of elephant moods and personal space, as these are wild animals being rehabilitated.
Use 'Assalamu alaikum' (peace be upon you) and Bahasa Indonesia phrases with staff; tipping is appreciated but not obligatory.
Understand that the center operates as an educational facility, not entertainment; your visit supports habitat protection and anti-poaching efforts for wild Sumatran elephants.

restaurants.name: Café Nuansa
restaurants.description: A beloved local café serving authentic Lampung dishes with a casual, welcoming atmosphere popular with families and office workers.
restaurants.mustOrder: Pempek Lampung with sweet and spicy sauce

restaurants.name: Warung Soto Banjar
restaurants.description: A no-frills warung specializing in traditional Banjarese soto and rice dishes, known for generous portions and authentic flavors.
restaurants.mustOrder: Soto Banjar with beef and turmeric broth

restaurants.name: Restaurant Chop Suey
restaurants.description: An established Chinese restaurant serving Cantonese-influenced dishes with both local and traditional dim sum in a comfortable family setting.

restaurants.name: Graha Raya Seafood
restaurants.description: A mid-range seafood restaurant with fresh daily catches prepared in Indonesian and Asian styles, popular for group dinners and celebrations.
restaurants.mustOrder: Grilled whole snapper with sambal matah

restaurants.name: Depot Bakso Mang Dadang
restaurants.description: A simple, long-running depot beloved for its rich, slow-cooked meatball broth and chewy noodles served in a humble setting.
restaurants.mustOrder: Bakso kuah besar with beef balls and innards

restaurants.name: Pelangi Seafood Restaurant
restaurants.description: A waterfront seafood venue in South Lampung offering fresh fish and crustaceans with views over the harbor, known for quality ingredients.
restaurants.mustOrder: Udang bakar (grilled prawns) with garlic butter

restaurants.name: Rumah Makan Sari Rasa
restaurants.description: A mid-range family restaurant in Metro serving regional Lampung specialties and Indonesian comfort food in a clean, air-conditioned space.
restaurants.mustOrder: Nasi kuning with chicken satay and tempeh goreng

restaurants.name: Warung Gadogado Bu Tini
restaurants.description: A street-level warung famous for its creamy, slightly sweet gado-gado prepared fresh to order with crispy crackers and peanut sauce.
restaurants.mustOrder: Gado-gado with extra peanut sauce and crackers

restaurants.name: Seafood 88
restaurants.description: An upscale seafood restaurant offering premium fresh fish, lobster and crustaceans with refined preparation and attentive service.
restaurants.mustOrder: Lobster thermidor or grilled fish with herb butter

restaurants.name: Nasi Goreng Kampung Pak Haji
restaurants.description: A humble stall with a loyal following, serving fragrant, expertly-wok-tossed kampung-style fried rice with optional protein.
restaurants.mustOrder: Nasi goreng kampung with shrimp paste and fried egg

restaurants.name: Café Lampung Heritage
restaurants.description: A cultural café celebrating Lampung's culinary traditions with modern plating, featuring regional dishes and local coffee in a thoughtfully designed space.
restaurants.mustOrder: Gulai kepala ikan (fish head curry) with pempek

restaurants.name: Restaurant Mak Cun
restaurants.description: A well-regarded spot in West Lampung serving Lampung highlands cuisine with emphasis on fresh local produce and traditional cooking methods.

restaurants.name: Warung Terang Bulan
restaurants.description: A casual street warung famous for its martabak and sweet pancakes, perfect for breakfast or late-night snacks with local flavors.
restaurants.mustOrder: Martabak telur with meat and egg filling

restaurants.name: Restaurant Dengan Sela
restaurants.description: A relaxed beachside restaurant in South Lampung offering fresh grilled fish and seafood with views of the Sunda Strait.
restaurants.mustOrder: Ikan bakar (grilled fish) with sambal and rice

restaurants.name: Kedai Kopi Lampung Jaya
restaurants.description: A traditional coffee shop serving Lampung's signature robusta coffee alongside simple snacks and breakfast items in an authentic local setting.
restaurants.mustOrder: Kopi Lampung with roti bakar and kaya jam
